assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: '| Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met |'. Detailed findings in report.md list all 5 criteria individually as PASS with code-level evidence (lines 90-103). criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each show 'Verdict: PASS' covering single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape respectively.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: '| Scope Containment | PASS | PR files match task specification exactly: 2 modified files and 1 new file, all matching Files to Modify/Create |'. Detailed findings (lines 30-36) provide file-by-file comparison: 'Task Files to Modify: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs, modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs', 'Task Files to Create: tests/api/package.rs', 'PR files: list.rs (modified), service/mod.rs (modified), tests/api/package.rs (new)', 'Out-of-scope files: none', 'Unimplemented files: none'.

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table line 5: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments or review body items exist on this PR |'. Line 6: '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate |'. No sub-task creation is mentioned anywhere in the outputs.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: All output files (report.md, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md) are purely analysis and reporting documents. The report references code via quotation and analysis but does not modify any files. No output file contains language indicating code modification (no 'applied fix', 'modified file', 'wrote to', or 'edited' statements). The outputs directory contains only markdown report files.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md concludes with '### Overall: PASS' and 'All checks pass. The PR fully implements the license filter feature as specified in TC-9101 with no issues requiring attention.' There is no mention of merging, auto-merging, approving, or taking any merge action anywhere in report.md or the criterion files. The report presents verdicts and evidence for human consumption.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: '| Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, API keys, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines across all 3 files |'. Detailed findings under Security section (lines 63-71): 'Scanned all added lines for hardcoded passwords, API keys, tokens, private keys, environment files, cloud credentials, and database credentials.' Per-file results: list.rs 'No sensitive patterns', service/mod.rs 'No sensitive patterns', tests/api/package.rs 'No sensitive patterns'.

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: '| Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | All test changes are in a newly created file (tests/api/package.rs); no existing tests modified or deleted |'. Detailed findings (lines 152-157): 'The only test file in the PR is tests/api/package.rs, which is newly created. No existing test files were modified or deleted. New test files are inherently additive.' Evidence: 'tests/api/package.rs: new file, 80 lines, 4 test functions, 0 removals'.

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: report.md detailed findings organized by four domains: Intent Alignment (lines 25-58) contains Scope Containment (PASS), Diff Size (PASS), Commit Traceability (PASS); Security (lines 60-71) contains Sensitive Pattern Scan (PASS); Correctness (lines 73-111) contains CI Status (PASS), Acceptance Criteria (PASS), Verification Commands (N/A); Style/Conventions (lines 113-157) contains Convention Upgrade (N/A), Repetitive Test Detection (PASS), Test Documentation (PASS), Eval Quality (N/A), Test Change Classification (ADDITIVE). Test Quality in summary table combines all three: 'All 4 test functions have doc comments; no repetitive tests detected (each test has distinct setup and assertions); Eval Quality: N/A'.

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 30-36): file-by-file scope comparison listing task files vs PR files with 'Out-of-scope files: none'. Security (lines 66-71): per-file scanning describing content of added lines ('Contains only SPDX import, struct field addition, validation function, and handler logic. No sensitive patterns.'). Correctness (lines 90-103): per-criterion code-level verification citing specific constructs (PackageListParams struct, validate_license_param, Condition::any(), is_in(), test assertions). Style/Conventions (lines 121-148): test quality assessment listing each test function with purpose, doc comments, and parameterization analysis.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: report.md lines 145-148: '#### Eval Quality -- N/A' with details: 'No eval result reviews were found on this PR (no reviews from github-actions[bot] with ## Eval Results marker and sdlc-workflow/run-evals footer).' Summary table line 13 shows Test Quality is PASS with 'Eval Quality: N/A' included, confirming N/A does not negatively affect the combined Test Quality verdict.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md lines 145-148: 'No eval result reviews were found on this PR'). Root-Cause Investigation in summary table: 'No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate'. No mention of eval failure sub-tasks anywhere in the outputs, consistent with Eval Quality being N/A rather than WARN.

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: report.md summary table: '| Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments or review body items exist on this PR |' and '| Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks were created; nothing to investigate |'. These two verdicts appear only in the top-level summary table and are NOT included under any of the four domain sections (Intent Alignment, Security, Correctness, Style/Conventions) in the Detailed Findings section, confirming they are determined independently from domain analysis.
